_comment: Fill in every field marked with TODO before running the demo.
This file is read exclusively by confluence_roundtrip_demo.py and
is completely separate from the project's main confluence_config.json.
base_url           : The root URL of your Confluence server, e.g. https://wiki.example.com
personal_access_token: In Confluence → Profile → Personal Access Tokens → Create token
source page_id     : Open any Confluence page, look at the URL: /pages/<number>/...
target parent_page_id: The page under which the new demo page will be created
space_key          : Short code shown in URLs and page breadcrumbs, e.g. 'TEAM'

confluence._comment: Connection details for your Confluence server
confluence._certificate_comment: Path to a PEM certificate file for self-signed or internal CAs. Leave blank to use system trust store.
confluence._verify_ssl_comment: Set to false ONLY for testing against servers with untrusted certificates — never in production.
confluence._timeout_comment: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Increase if your Confluence server is slow.

source_page._comment: The Confluence page the demo will READ data FROM. It must contain at least one HTML table.
source_page._page_id_comment: Numeric page ID from the Confluence URL: /pages/<ID>/page-title
source_page.description: Source page containing data to extract
_required_column_comment: Optional. If your page has multiple tables, name a column header that uniquely
identifies the table you want (e.g. 'Queue Manager', 'Application', 'Server').
Leave blank to use the first table on the page.

target_page._comment: The new Confluence page the demo will CREATE with the extracted data.
target_page._space_key_comment: The Confluence space key where the new page will be created, e.g. 'TEAM' or 'IT'.
target_page._parent_page_id_comment: The new page will appear as a child of this page.
target_page.title: Demo - Data Imported from Confluence
target_page._title_comment: Title of the new page. Change this to avoid naming conflicts.
target_page.description: Auto-generated demo page — safe to delete

output._comment: Where to save the extracted JSON file on disk
output._json_file_comment: Path relative to the project root. The directory is created automatically.

_shared_scripts_comment: Path to the directory containing confluence_client.py.
On RHEL this is /data/app/Scripts (installed by install.sh).
On Windows/dev machines, point this to the folder where confluence_client.py lives.
If confluence_client is already on sys.path or installed as a package, set this to ''.
